200.a: Sheldon Cooper, Leonard Hofstadter, Howard Wolowitz, and Raj Koothrappali

200.q: What is Sheldon's spot on the couch?
200.a: The middle seat on the left — it has the optimal sightlines and he never gives it up

200.q: Who plays Sheldon Cooper?
What is Sheldon's catchphrase?
200.a: Bazinga! — his way of saying he was joking

200.q: Who is Penny?
200.a: The pretty but not academically inclined neighbour who becomes Leonard's girlfriend and wife

200.q: What does Leonard do for work?
200.a: He is an experimental physicist at Caltech

200.q: What is Howard's notable distinction?
200.a: He is the only one without a PhD — he has a Masters in Engineering

200.q: What is Sheldon's field?
200.a: Theoretical physics — specifically string theory and later super-asymmetry

200.q: Who does Howard marry?
Who is Amy Farrah Fowler?
200.a: A neurobiologist who becomes Sheldon's girlfriend and eventually wife

400.a: A document outlining all rules and obligations of living with Sheldon — dozens of clauses

400.q: What is the Caltech?
400.a: The California Institute of Technology — where the main characters work

400.q: Who is Stuart?
400.a: The comic book store owner who becomes close to the group

400.q: What is Sheldon's IQ?
400.a: He claims 187 — though the show never officially confirms it

400.q: What Nobel Prize do Sheldon and Amy win?
400.a: The Nobel Prize in Physics — for their discovery of super-asymmetry

400.q: Who is Raj's dog?
400.a: Cinnamon — a Yorkshire Terrier he treats as his child

400.q: What is Soft Kitty?
400.a: A song Sheldon wants sung to him when he is sick — Warm kitty, soft kitty, little ball of fur

400.q: What is Howard's achievement?
400.a: He is the only one of the group to go to space — aboard the International Space Station

400.q: What does Raj's selective mutism prevent him from doing?
400.a: Talking to women he is not related to — unless he has alcohol (or thinks he does)

400.q: Who is Wil Wheaton in the show?
400.a: A recurring version of himself — initially Sheldon's nemesis, later a friend

What is the significance of the train set in Sheldon's childhood?
600.a: It represents the moment Sheldon's innocence was broken — he walked in on his father cheating

600.q: What is Sheldon's relationship with his faith?
600.a: His mother is deeply religious; Sheldon is atheist — a recurring tension in the show

600.q: What is the Young Sheldon connection?
600.a: A prequel spin-off showing Sheldon's childhood in Texas — it ran 7 seasons

600.q: What is the Big Bang Theory's most-watched episode?
600.a: The finale with 23 million viewers — when Sheldon and Amy win the Nobel Prize

600.q: What is the significance of Sheldon's three knocks?
600.a: He always knocks three times and says the person's name three times — a childhood anxiety habit

600.q: Who is Bernadette's dynamic with Howard?
600.a: She is the dominant partner — highly educated, assertive, and earns more than Howard

600.q: What is the Dungeons and Dragons dynamic in the show?
600.a: They play D&D regularly — it becomes a metaphor for their escapism and friendship

600.q: What is the significance of the elevator being broken?
600.a: The elevator is out of service for the entire show — finally fixed in the finale, symbolising change

600.q: What scientific concept does Sheldon explain most famously?
600.a: He frequently references string theory, Schrödinger's cat, and the theory of everything

600.q: What is Sheldon's relationship with food?
600.a: He has specific food days — Thai food on Mondays, pizza on Fridays — any change distresses him
